Former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=April 16th|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka city|label=Oyora Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oyosami-jinja, minami-torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] ``Oyora Shrine'' is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka City|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward|Q985702|WD=Q985702|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917326|lt=Fusha|1=Fu-sha}}. Currently, it is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=standalone shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} which does not belong to {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined God === The deities enshrined are as follows (the place names in parentheses are the old places before they were enshrined). [https://yosami-jinja.com/page-5741/ Oyora Shrine History]. * Main enshrined deity ** Taketoyo Hazurawake no Kimi - 9th {{ill|lt=Kaika Emperor|Q333548|WD=Q333548|1=Emperor Kaika}}{{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}}. ** {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sotsutsu no Onomikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} - One of the pillars of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. ** {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuno no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Nakatsutsu no Mikoto) - One of the pillars of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin. ** {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsu no Omikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Uwatsutsu no Onomikoto) - One of the three Sumiyoshi GodsOn the homepage of Oyora Shrine, it is listed as Omotzutsu no Omikoto. . * Deity enshrined ** {{ill|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Takamiko Omi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, (Kushimeigyoku no MikotoIt is listed as a deity enshrined on the home page of Oyora Shrine.) * Enshrined deity** Kusatsu Otoshi Okami (Shikiuchi Shrine, Karita), Nunotahime Okami (Shikiuchi Shrine, Sugimoto), {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Kenhaya Susano Okami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Abiko), {{ill|lt=Kushinada Hime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kinada Hime Daijin God|1=Kushinadahime}} (Sugimoto), Yabashira Miko Okami (Sugimoto), {{ill|lt=Oyama Kui no Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyama Kui Okami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (Niwai), {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susaki no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Sugimoto), Susaki no Mikoto (Yamanouchi) In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, which was established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), there are four enshrined deities. There are also four dais in the Aino-sai-jo under the Shiji-sai of the Engishiki, the Aino-sai-jo of the special festival, the {{ill|lt=Yasojima-sai|Q24870749|WD=Q24870749|lt=Yasojima-shin-sai|1=Yasojima Festival}} article, the Koriame-jin-sai-jo, and the Meishin-sai-jo. Kusatsu Otoshi Okami and the following were enshrined together in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}40). === About the enshrined deity === [[File:Oyosami-jinja, Yosamiike-ato.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Oyosami Pond Monument}}]]The name ``Ira'' of Oyora Shrine and its divine name refers to the place name {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In "{{ill|lt=Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Japanese Namesho|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}", {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi District|Q10885872|WD=Q10885872|1=Sumiyoshi district}} is located in Oyosamigo (near Oyora Shrine) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Province|1=UNKNOWN}} Irago (currently the northwestern part of {{ill|lt=Matsubara City|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}) can be seen in {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538|1=Tajihinokōri district}}, and it is said that the name of the place covered that area {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. It is assumed that ``Oyira Shrine'' was originally enshrined as a local deity in Yorami (Yira) {{Sfn|Oyira Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Regarding this Yomi area, there is an old article in the ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} period``Nihon Shoki'' written in October 62 of Emperor Sujin. ・{{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} period "Nihon Shoki" Emperor Suiko's 15th year (607) Koresaijo. shows that "{{ill|lt=Yorami Pond|Q65248853|WD=Q65248853|1=Yorami Pond}}" is built {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. This Iami Pond was a large-scale reservoir built by the royal power to cultivate farmland, but it was built in the first year of {{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=1704|1=UNKNOWN}}), most of them disappeared due to the {{ill|lt=Yamato River replacement|Q131618748|WD=Q131618748|lt=replacement work|1=replacement work}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}. However, part of it remained next to our company and came to be called Ajiemon Pond. After that, it was called Nii-no-Ike, but in {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485|1=1969}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}44) it was located next to {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefectural Hannan High School|Q11 441189|WD=Q11 441189|lt=Hannan High School}} It was reclaimed and disappeared as a ground [https://www.city.matsubara.lg.jp/docs/page4613.html Matsubara City History Walk 36 Construction of Imo Pond].In addition, "Nihon Shoki" {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} period"Nihon Shoki" September 43rd year of Emperor Nintoku, Koushi Sakujo. ・{{ill|lt=Emperor Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|lt=Emperor Kogyoku|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}Nihonshoki, May 5th, 642, the first year of Emperor Kogyoku. has a description of ``Yorami Tonkura'', and ``Wamyosho'' has a description of Miyake-go (present-day northern Matsubara City) in Tanbi District, Kawachi Province, and the area was expanded with the construction of Yorami Pond. link|Tunkura|Q11465761}} (ancient territory directly under the royal authority) was also established {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The Oyira deity enshrined there was also regarded as important by the imperial court, and as mentioned later, literature records that it received offerings from the imperial court many times.In addition, in the Yoreami area, it is known that the ancient clan '''{{ill|lt=Yorami Otarimi|Q24899491|WD=Q24899491|lt=Yora clan|1=Yosami no Otarumi}}''' (Yosamiuji, Yoreami clan/Yorami Abiko) resided {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. It appears that the Yira clan was involved in the management of Yorami Tunkura, and it is also speculated that he was a member of the Hosai clan of Oyora Shrine {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The current personal deity of Oyira-jin, Kenpōha Murawawakiō, appears in ``{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' as the ``progenitor of Yorimino Abi Kotono,'' and is a person who is positioned as the founder of the Ira clan.{{Sfn|Oyira Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. However, in ``{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'', Yira Sukune (Kobetsu of Settsu Province) is not listed as a descendant of Hikozamikoto}} (Prince of Emperor Kaika), rather than Kentoyo Hazurawaki, and there are discrepancies between documents {{Sfn|Ira clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. In addition, the same record also includes the Kamibetsu Yira Ren (Sakyo Jinbetsu and Ukyo Jinbetsu), Mononobe Yira Ren (Kawachi Kuni Jinbetsu), and Sone Shiya of {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428|lt=Baekje Country|1=Paekche}}. There are mentions of the descendants of Mamino-kun, such as Yiraren (from Kawachi Province), and it is said that there were multiple lines of clans that called themselves ``Yira'' {{Sfn|Ira clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. However, our company believes that the origin of the shrine is that the Yoragohiko clan enshrined their ancestor, King Kentohoha Murawaki, the fourth son of Emperor Kaika.Regarding the three Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto, Nakazutsuo no Mikoto, and Uetsutsuo no Mikoto) other than King Kentohohazurawaki, there is an article dated September 10, 9th year of the reign of Empress Jingu and Empress Chuai in the Pre-Sessho Period of the Nihon Shoki {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. In , when Empress Jingu conquered {{ill|lt=Silla|Q28456|WD=Q28456|1=Silla}}, she prayed to the three Sumiyoshi gods for victory and a safe voyage. At that time, there is a tradition that says that {{ill|lt=Yoramio Tarumi|Q24899491|WD=Q24899491|1=Yosami no Otarumi}} was appointed as the priest who enshrines the three Tsutsuo gods, and this is related to {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Furthermore, according to {{ill|lt=Early Modern Period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}},'' there used to be four deities to be enshrined: Ōmi Takamikoto, Tsukuyomi no Mikoto, Emperor Suinin, and Igoshigu (Igoshigu) (1977}}. Even after the four main deities enshrined today were established, the previous four continue to be enshrined in the main shrine . === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it was originally enshrined as a local deity in Yoriami, and it is presumed that it was enshrined by the Yira clan in ancient times {{Sfn|Oyira Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Furthermore, as mentioned above, the existence of the Yorami tonkura is recognized in the Yorami area, and there is a theory that Oyira Shrine functioned as a guardian deity when this tonkura was managed by the royal power {{Sfn|Oyira Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. === Outline === ==== Ancient ==== “{{ill|lt=Shinsho Kokufusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}” {{ill|lt=Daido (Heibonsha)|1986}}. After that, the divine rank was promoted to Shonii in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=909th year|Q24775|WD=Q24775|1=909}}) {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} |Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi District|Q10885872|WD=Q10885872|1=Sumiyoshi district}} has "Oyora Shrine 4-za" Nami-meishin University As part of the ``Monthly Aina Niiname'', 4 dais will be placed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, as well as {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{jalink |Ainamesai|Q11581944}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is written that it is kept on a heihaku {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In addition, in the Aijo Festival section under the Shijisai Festival of the ``Engishiki,'' and in the {{ill|lt=Yasojima Festival|Q24870749|WD=Q24870749|lt=Yasojima Shrine|1=Yasojima Festival}} section, Prayer Rain Festival section, and Meishin Festival section of the special festivals, Oyira-no-kami is mentioned among the gods who receive the heihaku. Among the place names that appear in ``{{ill|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyo Sho|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'' from the middle of the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, this area is compared to Oyosamigo, Sumiyoshi District.After that, the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Shoreki|Q1324858|WD=Q1324858|1=Shōryaku}} ({{ill|lt=994|Q24114|WD=Q24114|1=994}}) Volume 8] see frames 112-113). In , there is a hohei ceremony with Ujito Nakatomi as the Senmei envoy, and it is written in {{ill|lt=Kanhito|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951|1=Kannin}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249|1=1017}}) "Sakyoki" October 2nd year of Kannin 1st year (1017) ([{{NDLDC|1917934/29}} "Historical Materials Guide") (See National Diet Library Digital Collection frames 29-30). When Usa envoys were dispatched in , sacred treasures were dedicated to Oyira Shrine and other places {{Sfn|Oyira Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. {{ill|lt=Security (era name)|Q1194464|WD=Q1194464|lt=Security|1=Hōan}} In the 1st year ({{ill|lt=1120|Q19542|WD=Q19542|1=1120}}) of the "Settsu Province Tax Book Draft" and "Settsu Province Tax Book", regarding Kobe of Oyora Shrine, "Oyora Kobe Handling" It is written as ``100 rice temples, 2 and 10 collections'' {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. {{ill|lt=Eiman|Q1193868|WD=Q1193868|1=Eiman}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1165|Q19676|WD=Q19676|1=1165}}) It can also be found in the ``Annual Tribute Order for Jingikan Shrines'' {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. ==== Medieval/Early modern ==== In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the social power declined with the decline of the Yoriami clan, and the company affairs came to be handled by the {{ill|lt=Daisho Kannon-ji|Q11438537|WD=Q11438537|1=Daishō kannon-ji Temple}} (Fudo-in, Abiko Kannon) betto of {{ill|lt=Jingu-ji Temple|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In {{ill|lt=Early Modern Period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue|Q1135803|WD=Q1135803|1=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'', ``Ino Daimyojin'' is located in ``Abiko Village,'' its special place is Daisho Kannon-ji Temple, and the enshrined deity is unknown.{{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. According to the description in "Sumiyoshi Matsuba Daiki," the shrine was in serious decline at the time, and it is presumed that it was only able to survive with the support of Sumiyoshi Shrine. Also, in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1659|Q6995|WD=Q6995|1=1659}}), the main shrine, the shrine, and the hall of worship were destroyed by fire, so they were rebuilt {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. ==== Modern times and later ==== In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, it was separated from Daisho Kannon-ji Temple due to {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was initially listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}, but in February {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), |Q134917316}}, and in January 1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), it was designated as a Shinto shrine. Also, on November 1st of the same year, five nearby shrines were enshrined together: Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine), Abiko Shrine, Nunotahime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine), Yamauchi Shrine, and Doso Shrine. On October 1st, {{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}6), the company status was promoted to {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine 4th seat (shikinaisha)|1977}}. {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485|1=1969}} On September 29, 1969, the shrine building built in Manji 2 was destroyed by fire, so {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}} The shrine building was rebuilt with {{ill|lt=reinforced concrete structure|Q1577013|WD=Q1577013|1=reinforced concrete construction}} on October 3, 1977. === God level === * Records of divine investitures during the Six Kingdoms Historical Period ** On July 4, {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}}14 ({{ill|lt=847|Q30354|WD=Q30354|1=847}}), he was listed as a government shrine. name="July 14th year of Jowa">Article 4th of July, 847, "Shoku Nihon Koki" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). - The notation is "Oyorasha". ** {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Cheonan|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from Juvenile Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class ("{{ill|lt=Japan Three Generations Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}") New Year's Day 27th, 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jiroku" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). - The spelling is "Oyorajin". * After Six Kingdoms History ** September 13, {{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}}9 ({{ill|lt=909|Q24775|WD=Q24775|1=909}}), Sho 2nd Rank ("{{ill|lt=Nihon Kiryo|Q1053542|WD=Q1053542|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}") Article dated September 13, 909, of "Nihon Kiryaku" ([{{NDLDC|991095/405}} "Kokushi Taikei Volume 5"] see frame 405). - The spelling is "Oyorajin". === Precincts === * Main shrine - The current shrine building was built in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}} (Showa 44) after being destroyed by a fire on September 29, 1969|Q2485}} (Showa 44). Reconstruction of {{ill|lt=reinforced concrete structure|Q1577013|WD=Q1577013|1=reinforced concrete construction}} on October 3, 2007 {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine 4th seat (shikinaisha)|1977}}. Before it was destroyed by fire, it was built in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}}2 ({{ill|lt=1659|Q6995|WD=Q6995|1=1659}}) and consisted of the main shrine, the shrine, and the worship hall. It was built |Q210684}} with a {{ill|lt=kara gable|Q977293|WD=Q977293|1=Karahafu}}, and the roof was {{ill|lt=cypress bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. The old shrine building had a south-facing side near the north of the precincts, but during the reconstruction, it was changed to an east-facing side near the west side of the precincts. * Seiden * Hall of worship * Shrine office * Funeral hall * South gate* Ryūjii (Ryuujii) - In the southwest corner of the precincts, there is a well called ``Ryuujini'' (Ryuujini), and there used to be a well called ``Niwa'' on the north side of the precincts {{Sfn|Oyira Shrine 4-za (Shikinai Shrine)|1977}}. It is said that a dragon snake living in {{ill|lt=Yorami Pond|Q65248853|WD=Q65248853|1=Yorami Pond}} asked a farmer to remove the iron that had sunk in the pond, and in return, he promised rain if he offered water from the well and prayed. There is a legend that in the past, prayers for rain were actually held during times of drought. On the other hand, the garden can also be seen in the "Settsu Meisho Zue", and the place name of the enshrined area of ​​Oyora Shrine is also derived from this, but it is now buried and only a monument remains. * Kagura Hall - Enshrined Deity: Obihime Obihime ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}) * New Kagura Hall - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Amenouzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Tendenmemei|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * North Gate === Setsusha === * Shinmeisha - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime Omikami|1=Toyouke-hime}} * Tenjin Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hakuryu Okami * Ito Okami * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainatama Okami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Doso Shrine - Deities enshrined: Yatsuhiko Okami, Yatsuhihiko Okami, {{ill|lt=Kinokami|Q2963653|WD=Q2963653|lt=Kunato Okami|1=Kunado-no-Kami}} * Ryu Shrine - Enshrined Deity: Ryu Okami File:Oyosami-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts File:Oyora Shrine main hall.jpg|main hall File:Oyosami-jinja, Niwai-ato.jpg|Oyosami-jinja, Niwai-ato.jpg|Oyosami-jinja, Niwai-ato.jpg File:Oyosami-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Inarisha File:Oyosami-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinmeisha File:Oyosami-jinja, keidaisha-3.jpg|Doso Shrine === Festival === * Spring Festival (regular festival) (April 16th) * Summer Festival ** Yoi Matsuri (the day before the main festival) ** Main Festival (2nd Sunday of July) *Autumn festival ** Yoi Matsuri (the day before the main festival) ** Main Festival (2nd Sunday of October) === Appeared works === * A poem by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Teika|Q378425|WD=Q378425|1=Fujiwara no Teika}} from "Shui Guso" {{Cquote|{{Small|He reads the song that should be sung to Motoko, the priest of Yoriami, and reads it through the coffe}}
　Kimigayo is　　''Ira no Mori''　　pine and cedar and Chitosethriving|20px||Sada Fujiwara House, "Shui Gusou" {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine 4-za (Shikinaisha) | 1977}}{{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Gods) | 2000}}}} === About Goshu Shrine === Oyora Shrine was founded on November 1st, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40). Five shrines are enshrined together: Nunotahime Shrine (formerly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}), Yamauchi Shrine (formerly Murasha), and Doso Shrine (formerly Mukakusha). The next two shrines, Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine and Nunotahime Shrine, will become Shikinai Shrines {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine 4-za (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. ; Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine : ``Kusatsuooshijinja''. It was originally enshrined in Kanda, Niwa Village, Oaza Karita. : ``Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine Kuwasizuku'' in Sumiyoshi District, Settsu Province in the ``Engishiki'' divine name book, and ``Nakatomi Sumuji no Kami'' in the annotation of the Sumiyoshi deity {{ill|lt=Mikogami|Q352176|WD=Q352176|1=Mikogami}} of the Sumiyoshi deity {{ill|lt=Mikogami|Q352176|WD=Q352176|1=Mikogami}} in the ``Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki''. {{Sfn|Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}} (Akaru Hime no Mikoto Shrine, Shikinai Shrine Research Report Vol. 5, edited by Shikinai Shrine Research Group, Shikinai Shrine Research Group, 1977.). The entry ``Kuwazuku'' in the Shinmeicho means that the imperial court dedicated a hoe and a sewage bolt during the annual prayer festival. During the Meiji period, it was listed as a former village shrine, but was later enshrined with Oyora Shrine.: However, regarding ``Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine'' in the Shinmeicho, there is a theory that it is Otoshi Shrine, which is the outer branch shrine of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine, as another Hideron shrine (the ``Shikiuchi Shrine Investigation Report'' considers the former Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine in Karita to be more likely) {{Sfn|Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. ; Nunotahime Shrine : "Nunotahimejinja". It is said that it was originally enshrined in Sugimoto VillageAza Nukunota, but its location is currently unknown. : It is compared to ``Tsunotahime no Mikoto Shrine'' in Sumiyoshi District, Settsu Province in the ``Engishiki'' divine name book, and to ``Nunotahime no Kami'' of the Sumiyoshi no Mikogami in ``Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki.'' There is a theory that the god's name means the god of ``Nono-no-ta'' (rice fields) {{Sfn|Tsunotahime no Mikoto Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. During the Meiji period, it remained as a small shrine, and in 1872, it was listed as a former Mukakusha, but later it was enshrined with Oyora Shrine. === Local information === '''Location''' * Currently, it is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=standalone shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} which does not belong to {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Enshrined God === The deities enshrined are as follows (the place names in parentheses are the old places before they were enshrined). [https://yosami-jinja.com/page-5741/ Oyora Shrine History]. * Main enshrined deity ** Taketoyo Hazurawake no Kimi - 9th {{ill|lt=Kaika Emperor|Q333548|WD=Q333548|1=Emperor Kaika}}{{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}}. ** {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sotsutsu no Onomikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} - One of the pillars of {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. ** {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Nakatsutsuno no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Nakatsutsu no Mikoto) - One of the pillars of the Sumiyoshi Sanjin. ** {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsu no Omikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} (Uwatsutsu no Onomikoto) - One of the three Sumiyoshi GodsOn the homepage of Oyora Shrine, it is listed as Omotzutsu no Omikoto. . * Deity enshrined ** {{ill|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Takamiko Omi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|WD=Q595520|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Goju Takekami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Goju Takekami|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}, (Kushimeigyoku no MikotoIt is listed as a deity enshrined on the home page of Oyora Shrine.) * Enshrined deity** Kusatsu Otoshi Okami (Shikiuchi Shrine, Karita), Nunotahime Okami (Shikiuchi Shrine, Sugimoto), {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Kenhaya Susano Okami|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Abiko), {{ill|lt=Kushinada Hime|Q505469|WD=Q505469|lt=Kinada Hime Daijin God|1=Kushinadahime}} (Sugimoto), Yabashira Miko Okami (Sugimoto), {{ill|lt=Oyama Kui no Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Oyama Kui Okami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (Niwai), {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susaki no Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (Sugimoto), Susaki no Mikoto (Yamanouchi) In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, which was established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), there are four enshrined deities. There are also four dais in the Aino-sai-jo under the Shiji-sai of the Engishiki, the Aino-sai-jo of the special festival, the {{ill|lt=Yasojima-sai|Q24870749|WD=Q24870749|lt=Yasojima-shin-sai|1=Yasojima Festival}} article, the Koriame-jin-sai-jo, and the Meishin-sai-jo. Kusatsu Otoshi Okami and the following were enshrined together in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}40). === About the enshrined deity === [[File:Oyosami-jinja, Yosamiike-ato.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Oyosami Pond Monument}}]]The name ``Ira'' of Oyora Shrine and its divine name refers to the place name {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In "{{ill|lt=Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Japanese Namesho|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}", {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi District|Q10885872|WD=Q10885872|1=Sumiyoshi district}} is located in Oyosamigo (near Oyora Shrine) and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawachi Province|1=UNKNOWN}} Irago (currently the northwestern part of {{ill|lt=Matsubara City|Q774952|WD=Q774952|1=Matsubara}}) can be seen in {{ill|lt=Tanbi District|Q11368538|WD=Q11368538|1=Tajihinokōri district}}, and it is said that the name of the place covered that area {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. It is assumed that ``Oyira Shrine'' was originally enshrined as a local deity in Yorami (Yira) {{Sfn|Oyira Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Regarding this Yomi area, there is an old article in the ``{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} period``Nihon Shoki'' written in October 62 of Emperor Sujin. ・{{ill|lt=Emperor Suiko|Q298057|WD=Q298057|1=Empress Suiko}} period "Nihon Shoki" Emperor Suiko's 15th year (607) Koresaijo. shows that "{{ill|lt=Yorami Pond|Q65248853|WD=Q65248853|1=Yorami Pond}}" is built {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. This Iami Pond was a large-scale reservoir built by the royal power to cultivate farmland, but it was built in the first year of {{ill|lt=Hoei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=1704|1=UNKNOWN}}), most of them disappeared due to the {{ill|lt=Yamato River replacement|Q131618748|WD=Q131618748|lt=replacement work|1=replacement work}} of {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}. However, part of it remained next to our company and came to be called Ajiemon Pond. After that, it was called Nii-no-Ike, but in {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485|1=1969}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}44) it was located next to {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefectural Hannan High School|Q11 441189|WD=Q11 441189|lt=Hannan High School}} It was reclaimed and disappeared as a ground [https://www.city.matsubara.lg.jp/docs/page4613.html Matsubara City History Walk 36 Construction of Imo Pond].In addition, "Nihon Shoki" {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} period"Nihon Shoki" September 43rd year of Emperor Nintoku, Koushi Sakujo. ・{{ill|lt=Emperor Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|lt=Emperor Kogyoku|1=Empress Kōgyoku}}Nihonshoki, May 5th, 642, the first year of Emperor Kogyoku. has a description of ``Yorami Tonkura'', and ``Wamyosho'' has a description of Miyake-go (present-day northern Matsubara City) in Tanbi District, Kawachi Province, and the area was expanded with the construction of Yorami Pond. link|Tunkura|Q11465761}} (ancient territory directly under the royal authority) was also established {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The Oyira deity enshrined there was also regarded as important by the imperial court, and as mentioned later, literature records that it received offerings from the imperial court many times.In addition, in the Yoreami area, it is known that the ancient clan '''{{ill|lt=Yorami Otarimi|Q24899491|WD=Q24899491|lt=Yora clan|1=Yosami no Otarumi}}''' (Yosamiuji, Yoreami clan/Yorami Abiko) resided {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. It appears that the Yira clan was involved in the management of Yorami Tunkura, and it is also speculated that he was a member of the Hosai clan of Oyora Shrine {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. The current personal deity of Oyira-jin, Kenpōha Murawawakiō, appears in ``{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}'' as the ``progenitor of Yorimino Abi Kotono,'' and is a person who is positioned as the founder of the Ira clan.{{Sfn|Oyira Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. However, in ``{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'', Yira Sukune (Kobetsu of Settsu Province) is not listed as a descendant of Hikozamikoto}} (Prince of Emperor Kaika), rather than Kentoyo Hazurawaki, and there are discrepancies between documents {{Sfn|Ira clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. In addition, the same record also includes the Kamibetsu Yira Ren (Sakyo Jinbetsu and Ukyo Jinbetsu), Mononobe Yira Ren (Kawachi Kuni Jinbetsu), and Sone Shiya of {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428|lt=Baekje Country|1=Paekche}}. There are mentions of the descendants of Mamino-kun, such as Yiraren (from Kawachi Province), and it is said that there were multiple lines of clans that called themselves ``Yira'' {{Sfn|Ira clan (ancient clan)|2010}}. However, our company believes that the origin of the shrine is that the Yoragohiko clan enshrined their ancestor, King Kentohoha Murawaki, the fourth son of Emperor Kaika.Regarding the three Sumiyoshi deities (Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto, Nakazutsuo no Mikoto, and Uetsutsuo no Mikoto) other than King Kentohohazurawaki, there is an article dated September 10, 9th year of the reign of Empress Jingu and Empress Chuai in the Pre-Sessho Period of the Nihon Shoki {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. In , when Empress Jingu conquered {{ill|lt=Silla|Q28456|WD=Q28456|1=Silla}}, she prayed to the three Sumiyoshi gods for victory and a safe voyage. At that time, there is a tradition that says that {{ill|lt=Yoramio Tarumi|Q24899491|WD=Q24899491|1=Yosami no Otarumi}} was appointed as the priest who enshrines the three Tsutsuo gods, and this is related to {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Furthermore, according to {{ill|lt=Early Modern Period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549|WD=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}},'' there used to be four deities to be enshrined: Ōmi Takamikoto, Tsukuyomi no Mikoto, Emperor Suinin, and Igoshigu (Igoshigu) (1977}}. Even after the four main deities enshrined today were established, the previous four continue to be enshrined in the main shrine . === History === === Founding === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it was originally enshrined as a local deity in Yoriami, and it is presumed that it was enshrined by the Yira clan in ancient times {{Sfn|Oyira Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Furthermore, as mentioned above, the existence of the Yorami tonkura is recognized in the Yorami area, and there is a theory that Oyira Shrine functioned as a guardian deity when this tonkura was managed by the royal power {{Sfn|Oyira Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. === Outline === ==== Ancient ==== “{{ill|lt=Shinsho Kokufusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}” {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=According to the first year of Daido|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), in the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|WD=Q31216|1=765}}), {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private House)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe|1=Kanbe (social class)}} had a total of 18 houses, 8 from {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} and 10 from {{ill|lt=Bizen Province|Q1053688|WD=Q1053688|1=Bizen Province}} {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.In the {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=National History|1=Rikkokushi}}, in {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 14 ({{ill|lt=847|Q30354|WD=Q30354|1=847}}) , the shrine building of "Oyorasha" was repaired and designated as a government shrine, {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Tenan|1=Ten'an}} In January 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) , the {{ill|lt=Divine Rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of "Oyirajin" was promoted to the rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, and Eighth Grade ({{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1st year) September ``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', article dated September 8, 1st year of Jogan (859) (see {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). is a prayer for wind and rain, {{ill|lt=Genkei|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}) "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" article dated June 14, Genkei 1st year (877) (see {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). includes a prayer for sweet rain, Genkei 3 ({{ill|lt=879|Q25001|WD=Q25001|1=879}}) Article dated June 14, Genkei 3 (879) from "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" (see {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). contains an article about the dedication of sacred treasures {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. After that, the divine rank was promoted to Shonii in {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=909th year|Q24775|WD=Q24775|1=909}}) {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} |Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi District|Q10885872|WD=Q10885872|1=Sumiyoshi district}} has "Oyora Shrine 4-za" Nami-meishin University As part of the ``Monthly Aina Niiname'', 4 dais will be placed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, as well as {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{jalink |Ainamesai|Q11581944}} and {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, it is written that it is kept on a heihaku {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In addition, in the Aijo Festival section under the Shijisai Festival of the ``Engishiki,'' and in the {{ill|lt=Yasojima Festival|Q24870749|WD=Q24870749|lt=Yasojima Shrine|1=Yasojima Festival}} section, Prayer Rain Festival section, and Meishin Festival section of the special festivals, Oyira-no-kami is mentioned among the gods who receive the heihaku. Among the place names that appear in ``{{ill|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyo Sho|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'' from the middle of the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, this area is compared to Oyosamigo, Sumiyoshi District.After that, the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Shoreki|Q1324858|WD=Q1324858|1=Shōryaku}} ({{ill|lt=994|Q24114|WD=Q24114|1=994}}) Volume 8] see frames 112-113). In , there is a hohei ceremony with Ujito Nakatomi as the Senmei envoy, and it is written in {{ill|lt=Kanhito|Q1193951|WD=Q1193951|1=Kannin}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1017|Q19249|WD=Q19249|1=1017}}) "Sakyoki" October 2nd year of Kannin 1st year (1017) ([{{NDLDC|1917934/29}} "Historical Materials Guide") (See National Diet Library Digital Collection frames 29-30). When Usa envoys were dispatched in , sacred treasures were dedicated to Oyira Shrine and other places {{Sfn|Oyira Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. {{ill|lt=Security (era name)|Q1194464|WD=Q1194464|lt=Security|1=Hōan}} In the 1st year ({{ill|lt=1120|Q19542|WD=Q19542|1=1120}}) of the "Settsu Province Tax Book Draft" and "Settsu Province Tax Book", regarding Kobe of Oyora Shrine, "Oyora Kobe Handling" It is written as ``100 rice temples, 2 and 10 collections'' {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. {{ill|lt=Eiman|Q1193868|WD=Q1193868|1=Eiman}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1165|Q19676|WD=Q19676|1=1165}}) It can also be found in the ``Annual Tribute Order for Jingikan Shrines'' {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. ==== Medieval/Early modern ==== In the {{ill|lt=Middle Ages|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, the social power declined with the decline of the Yoriami clan, and the company affairs came to be handled by the {{ill|lt=Daisho Kannon-ji|Q11438537|WD=Q11438537|1=Daishō kannon-ji Temple}} (Fudo-in, Abiko Kannon) betto of {{ill|lt=Jingu-ji Temple|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. In {{ill|lt=Early Modern Period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}'s ``{{ill|lt=Wakan Sansai Zue|Q1135803|WD=Q1135803|1=Wakan Sansai Zue}}'', ``Ino Daimyojin'' is located in ``Abiko Village,'' its special place is Daisho Kannon-ji Temple, and the enshrined deity is unknown.{{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. According to the description in "Sumiyoshi Matsuba Daiki," the shrine was in serious decline at the time, and it is presumed that it was only able to survive with the support of Sumiyoshi Shrine. Also, in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1659|Q6995|WD=Q6995|1=1659}}), the main shrine, the shrine, and the hall of worship were destroyed by fire, so they were rebuilt {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. ==== Modern times and later ==== In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, it was separated from Daisho Kannon-ji Temple due to {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. In the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was initially listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}, but in February {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} (Meiji 9), |Q134917316}}, and in January 1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40), it was designated as a Shinto shrine. Also, on November 1st of the same year, five nearby shrines were enshrined together: Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine), Abiko Shrine, Nunotahime Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine), Yamauchi Shrine, and Doso Shrine. On October 1st, {{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}6), the company status was promoted to {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine 4th seat (shikinaisha)|1977}}. {{ill|lt=1969|Q2485|WD=Q2485|1=1969}} On September 29, 1969, the shrine building built in Manji 2 was destroyed by fire, so {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}} The shrine building was rebuilt with {{ill|lt=reinforced concrete structure|Q1577013|WD=Q1577013|1=reinforced concrete construction}} on October 3, 1977. === God level === * Records of divine investitures during the Six Kingdoms Historical Period ** On July 4, {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}}14 ({{ill|lt=847|Q30354|WD=Q30354|1=847}}), he was listed as a government shrine. name="July 14th year of Jowa">Article 4th of July, 847, "Shoku Nihon Koki" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). - The notation is "Oyorasha". ** {{ill|lt=Cheonan (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Cheonan|1=Ten'an}} January 27, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from Juvenile Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class ("{{ill|lt=Japan Three Generations Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}") New Year's Day 27th, 1st year of Jogan (859) in "Nihon Sandai Jiroku" (Refer to {{Harvnb|Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection}}). - The spelling is "Oyorajin". * After Six Kingdoms History ** September 13, {{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}}9 ({{ill|lt=909|Q24775|WD=Q24775|1=909}}), Sho 2nd Rank ("{{ill|lt=Nihon Kiryo|Q1053542|WD=Q1053542|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}") Article dated September 13, 909, of "Nihon Kiryaku" ([{{NDLDC|991095/405}} "Kokushi Taikei Volume 5"] see frame 405). - The spelling is "Oyorajin". === Precincts === * Main shrine - The current shrine building was built in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}} (Showa 44) after being destroyed by a fire on September 29, 1969|Q2485}} (Showa 44). Reconstruction of {{ill|lt=reinforced concrete structure|Q1577013|WD=Q1577013|1=reinforced concrete construction}} on October 3, 2007 {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine 4th seat (shikinaisha)|1977}}. Before it was destroyed by fire, it was built in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}}2 ({{ill|lt=1659|Q6995|WD=Q6995|1=1659}}) and consisted of the main shrine, the shrine, and the worship hall. It was built |Q210684}} with a {{ill|lt=kara gable|Q977293|WD=Q977293|1=Karahafu}}, and the roof was {{ill|lt=cypress bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}. The old shrine building had a south-facing side near the north of the precincts, but during the reconstruction, it was changed to an east-facing side near the west side of the precincts. * Seiden * Hall of worship * Shrine office * Funeral hall * South gate* Ryūjii (Ryuujii) - In the southwest corner of the precincts, there is a well called ``Ryuujini'' (Ryuujini), and there used to be a well called ``Niwa'' on the north side of the precincts {{Sfn|Oyira Shrine 4-za (Shikinai Shrine)|1977}}. It is said that a dragon snake living in {{ill|lt=Yorami Pond|Q65248853|WD=Q65248853|1=Yorami Pond}} asked a farmer to remove the iron that had sunk in the pond, and in return, he promised rain if he offered water from the well and prayed. There is a legend that in the past, prayers for rain were actually held during times of drought. On the other hand, the garden can also be seen in the "Settsu Meisho Zue", and the place name of the enshrined area of ​​Oyora Shrine is also derived from this, but it is now buried and only a monument remains. * Kagura Hall - Enshrined Deity: Obihime Obihime ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}) * New Kagura Hall - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Amenouzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Tendenmemei|1=Ame-no-Uzume}} * North Gate === Setsusha === * Shinmeisha - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime Omikami|1=Toyouke-hime}} * Tenjin Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Hakuryu Okami * Ito Okami * Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainatama Okami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Doso Shrine - Deities enshrined: Yatsuhiko Okami, Yatsuhihiko Okami, {{ill|lt=Kinokami|Q2963653|WD=Q2963653|lt=Kunato Okami|1=Kunado-no-Kami}} * Ryu Shrine - Enshrined Deity: Ryu Okami File:Oyosami-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts File:Oyora Shrine main hall.jpg|main hall File:Oyosami-jinja, Niwai-ato.jpg|Oyosami-jinja, Niwai-ato.jpg|Oyosami-jinja, Niwai-ato.jpg File:Oyosami-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Inarisha File:Oyosami-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Shinmeisha File:Oyosami-jinja, keidaisha-3.jpg|Doso Shrine === Festival === * Spring Festival (regular festival) (April 16th) * Summer Festival ** Yoi Matsuri (the day before the main festival) ** Main Festival (2nd Sunday of July) *Autumn festival ** Yoi Matsuri (the day before the main festival) ** Main Festival (2nd Sunday of October) === Appeared works === * A poem by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Teika|Q378425|WD=Q378425|1=Fujiwara no Teika}} from "Shui Guso" {{Cquote|{{Small|He reads the song that should be sung to Motoko, the priest of Yoriami, and reads it through the coffe}}
　Kimigayo is　　''Ira no Mori''　　pine and cedar and Chitosethriving|20px||Sada Fujiwara House, "Shui Gusou" {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine 4-za (Shikinaisha) | 1977}}{{Sfn|Oyora Shrine (Gods) | 2000}}}} === About Goshu Shrine === Oyora Shrine was founded on November 1st, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40). Five shrines are enshrined together: Nunotahime Shrine (formerly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mukakusha|1=UNKNOWN}}), Yamauchi Shrine (formerly Murasha), and Doso Shrine (formerly Mukakusha). The next two shrines, Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine and Nunotahime Shrine, will become Shikinai Shrines {{Sfn|Oyora Shrine 4-za (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. ; Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine : ``Kusatsuooshijinja''. It was originally enshrined in Kanda, Niwa Village, Oaza Karita. : ``Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine Kuwasizuku'' in Sumiyoshi District, Settsu Province in the ``Engishiki'' divine name book, and ``Nakatomi Sumuji no Kami'' in the annotation of the Sumiyoshi deity {{ill|lt=Mikogami|Q352176|WD=Q352176|1=Mikogami}} of the Sumiyoshi deity {{ill|lt=Mikogami|Q352176|WD=Q352176|1=Mikogami}} in the ``Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki''. {{Sfn|Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}} (Akaru Hime no Mikoto Shrine, Shikinai Shrine Research Report Vol. 5, edited by Shikinai Shrine Research Group, Shikinai Shrine Research Group, 1977.). The entry ``Kuwazuku'' in the Shinmeicho means that the imperial court dedicated a hoe and a sewage bolt during the annual prayer festival. During the Meiji period, it was listed as a former village shrine, but was later enshrined with Oyora Shrine.: However, regarding ``Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine'' in the Shinmeicho, there is a theory that it is Otoshi Shrine, which is the outer branch shrine of Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine, as another Hideron shrine (the ``Shikiuchi Shrine Investigation Report'' considers the former Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine in Karita to be more likely) {{Sfn|Kusatsu Otoshi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. ; Nunotahime Shrine : "Nunotahimejinja". It is said that it was originally enshrined in Sugimoto VillageAza Nukunota, but its location is currently unknown. : It is compared to ``Tsunotahime no Mikoto Shrine'' in Sumiyoshi District, Settsu Province in the ``Engishiki'' divine name book, and to ``Nunotahime no Kami'' of the Sumiyoshi no Mikogami in ``Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki.'' There is a theory that the god's name means the god of ``Nono-no-ta'' (rice fields) {{Sfn|Tsunotahime no Mikoto Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1977}}. During the Meiji period, it remained as a small shrine, and in 1872, it was listed as a former Mukakusha, but later it was enshrined with Oyora Shrine. === Local information === '''Location''' *
